signals.title: NZD/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Shift
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5873 — USD outperformance (1W avg +0.564%) vs NZD laggard status (1W avg -0.903%) creates a 1.467% G8 spread, the widest in the complex. The move is accelerated by a pivot in geopolitical risk as Ukraine/Russia headlines suggest a potential path to peace, reducing the 'commodity-beta' bid for NZD while Fed's Schmid reaffirms inflation as the 'biggest risk,' supporting the USD 150bp carry advantage. NZD real rate at -0.83% vs USD +1.23% confirms structural downside. Watch for a break of 0.5850 to accelerate the trend.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Vol + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.99 — Price is testing the critical 160.00 psychological level previously associated with BoJ intervention. JPY COT LF net short -86,249 (31.6% long) indicates a crowded carry trade, while the 25d RR at -1.15 (moderate put skew) shows the options market is aggressively hedging for JPY strength/USD downside despite the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage. JPY CESI at +11 suggests domestic data outperformance is providing a fundamental floor. Sustained trade above 160.00 triggers immediate intervention risk.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7140 — COT LF net long +58,041 (76.1% long) represents a positioning extreme, while retail sentiment is 70% short, creating a sharp institutional vs retail divergence. AUD IV at 17.0% vs HV30 at 8.1% (VRP +8.9%) signals an extreme volatility premium, suggesting the market is paying heavily for protection against a trend reversal. Negative AUD CESI (-8) contradicts the bullish spec positioning, raising the risk of a long-unwind. Watch for a break of 0.7100 to confirm a positioning flush.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + Carry Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1617 — IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 3.8% (VRP +3.3%) indicates elevated hedging demand despite a relatively stable spot price. The 175bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%) remains the primary anchor, though the ECB's hike bias vs Fed's hold stance creates a policy divergence. EUR CESI (+6) vs USD CESI (+26) shows both economies are outperforming expectations, but the USD real rate (+1.23%) remains significantly more restrictive than EUR (-0.26%). Watch the ECB meeting on 11 Jun for a hawkish shift to compress the carry spread.
